title: String Slices in Rust
content:
### String Slices in Rust

`&str` (pronounced "string slice") is a reference to a sequence of UTF-8 bytes. It is the preferred string type for function parameters.

---

### &str vs String

| Aspect | `&str` | `String` |
|--------|--------|----------|
| Memory | Points to existing data | Owned heap buffer |
| Mutability | Immutable | Mutable |
| Creation | Literals are `&str` | `String::from()` |
| Parameter | Preferred for function args | Use when ownership needed |

---

### String Slice Methods

```rust
let s = "Hello, Rust!";

// Length and inspection
println!("{}", s.len());         // byte length
println!("{}", s.is_empty());    // false
println!("{}", s.contains("Rust"));  // true

// Slicing
let hello = &s[0..5];   // "Hello"

// Methods returning &str
let trimmed = "  text  ".trim();     // "text"
let words: Vec<&str> = s.split(" ").collect();
let upper = s.to_uppercase();         // returns String

// Checking prefixes/suffixes
s.starts_with("Hello");  // true
s.ends_with("Rust!");    // true

// Finding
s.find('R');  // Some(7)
```

> **Key Point:** `&str` is the idiomatic parameter type for functions that need string data. It can accept both `&String` (via deref coercion) and string literals. String slices operate on byte indices, not character indices.

quiz.question: What type do string literals have in Rust?
